articles.headline: World Cup Last Dance: Courtois, Belgium's golden goalkeeper, gets one more go
articles.description: Belgium's 'Golden Generation' were the world's No. 1 team, but didn't win a trophy to show for it. Now, Thibaut Courtois is one of the last ones left.

images.credit: Steph Chambers - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Tielemans: Belgium coming to the World Cup to win
images.caption: Youri Tielemans is targeting World Cup glory as his Belgium side travel to their Seattle base camp.

articles.headline: Rod Stewart pictured at Scotland World Cup win shortly after cancelling concert due to illness
articles.description: Sir Rod Stewart was seen celebrating Scotland's World Cup win over Haiti at Gillette Stadium in Boston on Saturday night shortly after cancelling a concert due to illness.

competitions.altGameNote: FIFA World Cup, FIFA World Cup

commentary.text: Who will snatch third place in the 2010 World Cup? Check out our preview for all the crucial team news and facts.
Join us at 18.30 GMT as Germany take on Uruguay.
Both teams have put strong teams out for tonight's game but the big team news is that record-chasing Miroslav Klose misses out for a flu-hit Germany.
ESPN Stats & Info: This will be the Uruguayans' third time in the third-Place match, having already lost to Austria (3-1 in 1954) and Germany (1-0 in 1970).
ESPN Stats & Info: Germany have played in four World Cup third-Place matches, winning three.
Klose is just one goal behind Ronaldo's goalscoring record for the World Cup (15) and was seen warming up so he might feature off the bench.
Muller returns from suspension and Jansen starts on the left in place of flu-stricken Podolski.
For Uruguay, skipper Lugano returns at centre-back, along with Godin.
ESPN Stats & Info: No World Cup third-place match has ever gone to penalties.
Miroslav Klose warms up prior to kick-off
Sepp Blatter has shaken hands with all the players and now it is time for the national anthems.
The Uruguay players looked up for this match during their anthem. Now it is the turn of Germany.
The flag waving and chest pumping is over and now it is time for the football. With the pressure off in this play-off let's hope for a good game.

ESPN Stats & Info: Paul the Octopus has correctly determined the outcome of every Germany match (including an upset loss to Serbia) at this World Cup. It was again called upon and selected Germany to win this match.
Paul has only been wrong once since Euro 2008 and the stats back him up as the last seven third-place matches have been won by European teams.
Uruguay get the game underway, kicking from left to right, wearing all light blue. Germany are in all black.
Germany enjoy the early possession but Muller's attempted cross is picked off.
Schweinsteiger is brought down by Fucile so Germany have a chance to sling the ball into the box. Muller puts the ball in the net but the flag is up for offside.
Aogo takes a heavy touch and seriously fouls Perez trying to win the ball back. Yellow card from the ref but it should have been red.
Aogo went over the top of the ball, but luckily Perez can continue.
Forlan hits a freekick straight into the wall. The ref has blown for a handball in the wall so Forlan wil get a second chance.
Forlan dinks a shot over the wall but his effort doesn't dip enough to drop into the far corner of the net... good effort though.
Mertesacker makes a good tackle on Suarez. Germany are on the attack. Aogo disguises a cross and plays a good ball in for Schweinsteiger, who wins a corner.
Uruguay defend it at the expense of another corner.
Ozil delivers the corner and Friedrich heads against the bar. So close for Germany!
Uruguay are hoping to defy the prediction of Paul the Octopus
ESPN Stats & Info: With 13 goals, Germany are the highest-scoring team at this World Cup so far.
Fucile darts past two German defenders and then goes down looking for a freekick but the ref correctly waves play on.
Muller combines with Ozil, whose shot is blocked. Uruguay attack but give the ball away very cheaply.
Caceres switches the play from left to right. Cavani hits a cross into the box on the volley but there is nobody in there for Uruguay.
Fucile has pushed forward to try and get things going for Uruguay but their final ball is invariably a poor one. No way through as yet.
Bastian Schweinsteiger fights with Jorge Fucile
GOAL Schweinsteiger takes a shot that Muslera can only parry and Muller taps in the rebound.
That is Muller's fifth goal of the World Cup and he is tied with the likes of Spain's David Villa for the Golden Boot.
Uruguay were just getting into the game when that goal came.
Forlan and Suarez combine, but a heavy touch from the former means the move breaks down. Uruguay's three forwards are very isolated at times.
ESPN Stats & Info: In their record 99th World Cup match, Germany have scored the opening goal for the 60th time.
Germany on the attack again. Jansen whips in a cross that takes a deflection and is claimed by goalkeeper Muslera.
Plenty of possession but not cutting edge once again for Uruguay.
Suarez's shot is blocked and loops up in the air. Forlan is waiting to head it in but Mertesacker blocks the striker's effort.
Friedrich clears from under his own bar from the resulting corner.
Muller to Ozil, who crosses for Khedira. It was a flowing move but the flag is up for offside.
GOAL Cavani stabs the ball in for Uruguay.
Suarez played the ball left for Cavani, who took a heavy touch but managed to recover in time to poke the ball past Butt in the German goal.
ESPN Stats & Info: If results hold, Thomas Müller will win the Golden Boot over Villa (ESP)/Sneidjer (NED) based on more assists.
Suarez, who is being booed at every touch, cuts inside and plays the ball into Cavani, who turns in the box but just can't get a shot off. Nice move.
Jansen plays a pass down the line for Ozil, who crosses. Lugano stumbles as he clears, but clears he does.
Perez wins back possession for Uruguay. Caceres wins a corner.
Forlan takes the corner and Mertesacker heads it clear.
Thomas Muller slots the ball past Fernando Muslera
Muller and Schweinsteiger combine in midfield. Muller plays a throughball for Ozil to chase but it is cut out by the Uruguay defence. Good football though.
Then rain is really pouring down in Port Elizabeth.
Jansen looks down at the sodden pitch after shanking a cross into row Z following some great build-up play down the left flank.
ESPN Stats & Info: Cavani's goal was the first Germany has allowed in the first half hour at this World Cup.
ESPN Stats & Info: Germany have already taken more shots in the match than they managed against Spain in the semi-final.
Forlan drives forward and rides a strong challenge from Friedrich on the edge of the box, but the striker can't keep control of the ball.
Forlan plays a great throughball for Suarez, who looks up and then shoots wide of the far post from the tight angle.
Edinson Cavani celebrates his goal
Schweinsteiger takes a freekick. His shot hits the wall and goes behind for a corner.
Schweinsteiger takes the corner. Fucile turns the ball behind for another corner. This time Uruguay clear and go on the attack...
...Pereira runs at goal. He twists and turns but Mertesacker sticks with his man and makes a good tackle in the box.
Schweinsteiger lofts the ball into the box. Friedrich sets himself for a spectacular volley but he is a centre-back and totally messes it up.
That's the final action of the first-half as the ref blows for half-time.
Well it was an enjoyable first-half with a nice ebb and flow as each side enjoyed a spell of supremacy.
Germany's Muller will certainly be up for the fight in the second-half with the Golden Boot at stake and Uruguay's Suarez will want to silence all those fans that are booing him.

Spain's David Villa and Netherlands' Wesley Sneijder both have five goals too in the race for the Golden Boot and they go head to head in Sunday's final, that you can also follow on Soccernet. To get you in the mood here is the preview.

ESPN Stats & Info: These two teams have combined to score 13 second-half goals in the tournament but Germany have conceded only one after half-time - the goal that eliminated them against Spain in the semi-final.
Germany get the second-half underway. No changes by either side.
Jansen wins a corner off Fucile. Schweinsteiger whips in the delivery and Muslera is saved by one of his defenders.
Cavani drives into the box and Butt rushes out to save his shot. The ball drops to Suarez, whose follow-up shot is again saved by Butt from close-range.
Great goalkeeping that from Butt, but Suarez should have given him no chance to save the second shot.
Schweinsteiger takes down Forlan before the striker cna launch a counter-attack. No yellow card from the ref though.
GOAL Arevalo drives down the right and plays a great one-two on the flank before whipping in a cross that Forlan volleys in from the edge of the box.
That was great goal from Uruguay. A brilliant build up and a great finish. Forlan is now on five goals too... bring on the Golden Boot?
Cavani attacks to keep Uruguay on the front foot.
Khedira is crowded out by three defenders as he attempts to create an opening on the right flank.
GOAL Jansen heads in at the back post after Muslera flapped at a cross.
You have to blame the goalkeeper for that one. But it makes for an interesting game.
ESPN Stats & Info: Germany have not lost a game at this World Cup when holding a lead in the match. (4 wins)
Diego Forlan has five goals in the World Cup
Cacau and Ozil probe the Uruguay defence but it holds firm.
Perez gets booked for rash challenge.
Cacau crosses and Godin clears acrobatically. Uruguay counter-attack and Butt is forced to make a good save from a long-range effort from Suarez.
Muslera picks the ball up after a touch from Fucile but the ref says it was not an intentional back pass.
Cavani attempts a one-two with Suarez, whose return pass takes a deflection off Schweinsteiger and drops for Forlan. Butt makes a good save from Forlan's shot.
Muller hits a shot from distance. He claims it took a deflection and should be a corner. The ref disagrees.
ESPN Stats & Info: Forlan now has 29 career goals, to put him second in Uruguay's all-time list, two behind Hector Scarone.
Diego Forlan scores his goal to make it 2-1 to Uruguay
Pereira is still battling hard and gives away a foul after rugby tackling Aogo.
Fernando Muslera looks on as Marcell Jansen celebrates
Cacau, who has been very quiet all match, shoots over the bar from 15-yards out.
Perez blocks the run of Ozil and Germany have a freekick. They take it quickly and it comes to nothing.
Speak of the devil. Cacau goes off and Kiesling comes on.
Will we see Klose at some point? He is only one goal off Ronaldo's World Cup record and has an eye on the Golden Boot himself.
Schweinsteiger has a chance to put the ball in the box but delays too long and Uruguay clear for a corner.
Kiesling turns on the left flank and then beats two players before stinging the hands of Muslera with a shot.
Perez, who has been booked, goes off and is replaced by Gargano.
Uruguay have a freekick within shooting distance. Pereira takes it, but blasts his shot over the wall and over the bar.
Arevalo flicks a lovely pass down the line for Cavani, but the striker has tired legs and can't make the most of it.
Boating whips in a great cross from the right. Kiesling seems to get caught in two minds as to use his foot or his head. In the end he uses neither and the chance goes begging.
Jansen goes off for Germany and is replaced by Kroos.
GOAL Khedira heads in after a quick game of pinball by the Uruguay defence in the box.
Uruguay have it all to do again now. Time is ticking away as Germany close on the bronze medal.
Suarez nutmegs Aogo on the flank but he can't pick out a team-mate with his cross.
Uruguay are pushing forward and might get caught on the break as Ozil threatens. A good tackle from Lugano snuffs out the danger.
Forlan is robbed by Boateng. Khedira plays a great pass through to Kiesling, who scoops his shot over the bar from inside the box. How did he miss the target?
Cavani goes off and is replaced by Abreu for Uruguay.
Fucile crosses for Abreu, who  has only just come on, but Mertesacker beats him to the header.
Germany want to make a change to run down the clock but Uruguay keep the ball in play as they look for an equaliser.
Germany make their chnage as Ozil goes off and Tasci gets his first taste of the World Cup.
Suarez backs into Friedrich and wins a freekick in shooting distance...
...Forlan whips the ball over the wall and his shot smacks the bar. So close!
The final whistle blows! Forlan was an inch away from a late equaliser and taking the lead in the race for the Golden Boot.
Germany have won the match 3-2 and claim the not-so-coveted third place at the World Cup.
The Germans are just the second team in this tournament to win after trailing in the second half and it is a fitting end for a side that has brought the most excitement to the World Cup.
Uruguay have also done exceptionally well at this World Cup and irrespective of this result head back to Montevideo for a party.
You can read a full report of the match here and also check out our post-match gallery and reaction... and don't forget to join us for the final.
